chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: قَالَ أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ فَزَّعَ رَجُلًا عَنِ الْجِدَارِ أَوْ نَفَّرَ بِهِ عَنْ دَابَّتِهِ فَخَرَّ فَمَاتَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِدِيَتِهِ وَ إِنِ انْكَسَرَ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِدِيَةِ مَا يَنْكَسِرُ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whichever man panics a man from the wall, or dislodges him from his animal, so he falls off and dies, so he is responsible for his wergild; and if he (the rider) breaks something, so he is responsible of the wergild of whatever has been broken from him’.
